title: Wagner's Valkyries Take Flight
story: Thunderous music filled the air as Richard Wagner's opera 'Die Walküre' soared onto the stage for the first time. In 1870, on June 26, audiences in Munich were swept away by the powerful sounds of 'Ride of the Valkyries'. This famous piece, with its dramatic horns and intense rhythm, became one of the most recognizable classical tunes ever written.
activity_question: What famous piece of music was part of Wagner's opera 'Die Walküre'?
activity_choices: Dance of the Sugar Plum Fairy
bonus_fact: 'Die Walküre' is part of Wagner's Ring Cycle, a series of four epic operas.

title: Heroes Honored with the Victoria Cross
story: Brave soldiers stood tall as Queen Victoria pinned shiny medals on their chests. On June 26, 1857, she awarded the first Victoria Crosses to 62 heroes who fought in the Crimean War. This special honor recognized their incredible bravery in battle. The Victoria Cross became the highest military award for valor in the British armed forces.
activity_question: What was the name of the medal awarded by Queen Victoria?
bonus_fact: The Victoria Cross is made from the metal of captured enemy cannons.

title: Disco Fever Takes Over Madison Square Garden
story: Disco mania reached new heights in 1979! The Village People, famous for their catchy tunes and colorful costumes, made history on June 26. They became the first disco group to perform at the legendary Madison Square Garden in New York City. Fans danced the night away to hits like 'Y.M.C.A.' and 'Macho Man' in the massive arena.
activity_question: What famous venue did The Village People perform at?
bonus_fact: The Village People's name comes from New York City's Greenwich Village neighborhood.

title: Bowling Brilliance at Lord's Cricket Ground
story: Imagine stepping onto the cricket pitch for the first time and becoming an instant legend! That's exactly what happened to Bob Massie in 1972. The Australian swing bowler made his Test cricket debut on June 26 at Lord's, the famous ground in England. Massie bowled with incredible skill, taking an amazing 16 wickets in just one match. His performance helped Australia defeat England in a stunning display of cricket mastery.
activity_question: Where did Bob Massie make his incredible Test cricket debut?

title: Babe Herman: Baseball's Hitting Machine
story: Swing, batter, batter! On June 26, 1903, a future baseball star was born. Babe Herman entered the world in Buffalo, New York, destined to become a legendary right fielder. With lightning-fast reflexes and a keen eye, Herman would go on to achieve an impressive .324 career batting average. He even hit for the cycle a record three times, a feat that left fans cheering and opponents scratching their heads.

title: Coaching Legend Tara VanDerveer Born
story: Slam dunk for women's basketball! Tara VanDerveer, a coaching superstar, entered the world on June 26, 1953. Born in Melrose, Massachusetts, she grew up to lead Stanford University to three NCAA National Championships. VanDerveer's golden touch extended to the Olympics, where she guided Team USA to victory in 1996.
